mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth is positive but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below a certain threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and strong consumer spending, boosting corporate profits and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 8.0% but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rate combined with strong housing starts indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting real estate activity, which has a broad stimulative effect on the economy and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If both FEDFUNDS and HOUST meet the trigger conditions, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial expansion, increasing industrial production signals robust manufacturing and supply chain activity, which translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving S&P 500 returns. This effect is stronger during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive during an economic expansion, but SPX 60-day returns show no statistically significant outperformance compared to baseline returns (p-value > 0.1) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable inflation rate, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, provides a favorable environment for corporate planning and consumer purchasing power, reducing uncertainty and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month change is below 3.0% but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, ultimately driving corporate earnings and S&P 500 performance over longer horizons.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and low federal funds rate indicates a robust economy supported by accommodative monetary policy, which fuels corporate expansion and investor confidence, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS meet the trigger conditions, but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts above a historical average indicates strong residential investment and construction activity, which has positive spillover effects across various sectors, boosting overall economic growth and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 700.0 but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year period.
